reviews.review_msg: There are moments of decency and care from Sarb, namely her interactions with students and quiz reductions in the second half of the term, but be warned that she is pretty overbearing – among the 7 chapters and 2 articles of final exam material in said second half, she slaps on two group projects; the final project is exhaustingly overdemanding.
She didnt explain properly the specific requirements for the course's projects and exam and when you ask for it, she just ignore the question, which is very bad. Not recommend:(((
Believe me Sarb is one the toughest prof in marking test. She only taught ppt in class which made me feel so boring throughout the course. If this is not require then don't choose her.

She might seem a fair and kind but a harsh grader, but she isn't. Despite her lies to be fair, she doesn't take her students concerns seriously. Don't wait good grades if you start on the wrong foot then get sick. She is only understands her A+ students. Don't take her class if you don't need to, you don't deserve that. Take care and good luck.

Sarb is one of the toughest professor, RUN! Her voice is not clear even if you sit in front, break time is just 10 minutes, she don't accept your opinion. Prepared for quiz, in class writing, readings, 2 exams, individual assignments, teamwork, & presentations! Overall, if this is mandatory then maybe take it, but if it's elective course then NO!
Great Instructor at all, all the BUS 200 students should take her classes!

You have to get her to like you otherwise it will be bad. She expects professionalism at all time which is confusing. You cant choose your group and its as *diverse as possible* and at the end some team members do nothing and that affects you but she does not understand it. You have to complete team documents every week.
Wonderful!!
Best professor you should choose without doubt at FIC! Perfect!
Excellent!
You will enjoy this wonderful and fun business course journey!!!
She is a wonderful professor!

She is an okay teacher (bit of karen vibes ngl) but as long as she likes you, you should be fine. Just remember that she makes you buy an 80 dollars textbook from pearsons for her class (none of my other class teachers have asked that from the students). so be prepared for that.
She is amazing!
Her class is not so interesting, but she will be an instructor that you feel willing to communicate with and learn from. I studied BUS 216 with her in 202301, so you can choose with confidence!
She is a very good professor and highly recommends you to choose to study with her! Best!

Well just make sure she likes you from the start. 
Her lectures are boring as she only reads off of ppt. She will not tell how to do the assignments exactly, only afterwards she explains how it should have been done. 
Does not bother to properly clarify questions.
Lastly, she kinda argues if you have a question which she finds not to her liking.
only taught ppt's in class and class was always boring. Explanation was not sufficient.
Real life example of a Karen.

Sarb is one of the finest professors I have seen. Though she is a tough grader, but if you come in her good books and participate in the class from the starting of the course, you will definitely get a solid A in her course. I would definitely take her in BUS216

You will clearly know what needed to do, deadline She will present with her PowerPoint which makes more clear. Mostly assignments, midterm and final exams are case study. Quizzes refer to the things she said in the lessons. Everything is clear. The marking is fair I think. I am not good in writing but my quizzes are quit good so B. Cry

She refuses to explain basic things saying that it isnt the way of the world Excuse me? Youre the teacher?? Oh and you give the quiz and then she explains the chapter and the slides are posted after as well

She is worst instructor that I have ever taken class at FIC . She doesnt care to provide good explanation for the assignments. And her instruction outline is not even clear . Take this class with any professor except her .

Sarb is actually a good instructir. She is very inspiring and prepares you for the corporate world. You just need to pay attention in class and always read the text book after the lectures. Before exams, go through the review slides she posts and youll be fine. She is amazing, just be respectful to her.

Sarb is a really good teacher. You need to ask her questions so she can give you the answers. You need to be hard working and she really likes students that have motivation so be ready to show your enthusiasm at first. Read the text book and the PowerPoint. These will give you good grade.

Sarb really cares about her students and wants you to do well - and it shows. She replies quickly and effectively to emails and questions, at all times of the day. If you apply yourself, you will do well in this class. Exams are based mostly on lecture material, but reading the textbook definitely helps understand concepts.

Sarb's classes are not easy but I think she is an excellent teacher for those who actually want to LEARN. She does not give away answers for exams like some teachers. You pay to LEARN not to get a passing grade, what would that serve you in the future. She uses good real life examples and tries to relate a lot of things to other classes.

Hard work in class  super low grades! Worst teacher! Doesnt give fair grades to deserving students and shell constantly find ways of criticizing you! SFU, please review students feedback on her. People deserve better.

SARB HOVEY DOES NOT RESPECT HER STUDENTS. SHE IS ALWAYS RUDE TO HER STUDENTS AND NOT A GOOD LISTENER. SHE WILL ALWAYS DEFEND HER SIDE AND WILL SHUT YOU UP INFRONT OF THE CLASS! SHE MAKES HER STUDENTS FEEL DUMB AND DOES NOT ENCOURAGE CLASS PARTICIPATION. SHE GIVES LOW GRADES TOO! DONT TAKE HER AS YOUR PROF!
Terrible prof, doesnt care about her students. She comes across as conceited and doesnt try to explain the material properly. If you question her once shell pick on you in class forever.

she is a nice person, you can ask lots of question and she is fine, but she is a tough grader and she always try to show she is doing fair. But boring classes is the thing that made me afraid of her class.
Her lectures are not interesting. A lot of memories. The harder you work on it, the better grade you get.

Sarb makes you work for the grade. She wants to know that you understand the content, and know how it comes into play into your current role.  You should be there to learn, not get an easy pass for your certificate.

Many Presentations and reports to do in her class but the main thing is she gives everyone almost the same grade. The class average was pretty low. Easy to get a C but hard to get B's or A's in her class. Her marking guide was hilarious.  Hard work does not deserve A's in her class.
The best thing about Sarb's class is that she's always there for you, if you regularly participate in class, and do the readings before hand you're good to go.

Sarb is a good teacher but she's tough The lectures are pretty long but only because she feels that everything in the text is important and should be included in the lecture. Attend all the classes, if possible - it will help on the exams. Papers are also tough as grammar and spelling counts. Good luck!

I found Sarb to be awesome! She provides inspirational material in class and I am quite engaged in her class. She is a fair grader, and I find she stays on topic with class discussions. I would definitely take another class of hers.
If I've read the reviews on this site before attending her classes, I wouldn't even register for the class. She's horrible in every  sense. Save your time and money.
Her classes are not as bad as the rating. She explains the content clearly and the exams are not too difficult. I think she is a good teacher

I checked the reviews on Sarb before enrolling in a class but I was left with no option in the end. I really expected the worst but I've learnt not to treat RMP ratings as the basic truth. She's not the greatest teacher in the world and one thing she said is that it is her job to teach and not entertain. If only she were more engaging

Absolutely the worst instructor I have ever had.  Not helpful, not approachable, condescending and rude.  Management from SFU should audit her class to see what a poor instructor she is.  I have avoided taking a number of classes in the HR certificate program to avoid having her again.
Boring, terrible instructor, read from book.  I spent good money on that class and its something I could have done at home on my own with the textbook.  Total waste of time.  I was very disappointed.

Very boring lectures, lack of practical examples during class even though it was asked for by students, lack of explanation on how to implement the learned material into work life. The lectures were purely read from the textbook, teacher didn't put in any effort to make class interesting and to actually teach us valuable things for the workplace.
very boring prof. lectures are very boring and she talks very very quietly
Her lectures are really boring, but the exams are super easy and straight from the textbook. Tutorials are useful and interesting. Super easy to get a good grade in this class.
Her voice is very monotonous and she speaks really quietly. Take this class with Chris if you can.
Very monotone lectures and teaches the same things that can be read in the textbook.

Horrible Professor. Incredibly boring and just made slides from the textbook. Lecture attendance was sometimes as low as 30/200. The power-points were helpful but she really needs to start being louder and more interesting.

I've been attending SFU for 2 semester but she is probably the worst professor I've ever had so far... and also one of the worst speakers also... None of my friends and group mates attend the class because the way she speaks makes her lecture a waste of your time. Class average is wayyyy lower than other BUS 272 classes STAY AWAY FROM HER
